rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN YEE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/02/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/18/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 01/23/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) Notice must be given to the Departments of State Hospitals and Health Care Services. The proof of service filed on 02/07/2024 shows notice given to them on 02/07/2024, less than 30 days before this hearing. 2) The total amount of the trustee fees (paragraph 9 & Exhibit B of petition) appears incorrect. 3) Legal costs paid on 3/2/22 & 12/21/22 (Schedule B) must be itemized. 4) Exhibit C shows a number of hours billed by multiple attorneys and paralegals for their internal communications within the firm. Is it reasonable to charge the trust for all of those communications?

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Lack Creek Rd., Lot 32, Shingletown, Ca 96088 (Apn 704-140-020-000))
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEMMA RUTH PAYBARAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 10/29/24. A petition for final distribution or a further report of status must be filed by 10/18/24. Appearance will be required on 10/29/24 if no petition is timely filed. NB: (1) The will was admitted in an order dated 6/15/20; the order was filed on 6/17/20. (2) Mercy Hall was appointed as GAL for Keivan Ryder Paybarah on 8/10/23; that order was filed on 8/11/23. (3) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] (4) Notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor, when the minor is 12 years old or older. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. (5) When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALIM UDDIN KHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse or domestic partner of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. (4) Another year for estate administration is too long if the only asset (in Michigan) is about to be sold, once the management company honors the administrator's authority. (5) Unless the other heirs waive an accounting, the first two-year accounting for the period 7/21/21 - 7/20/23 is overdue. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator is only now consolidating funds into one account...? The creditor claims period expired 5/25/23, four months after Letters were issued, not 7/21/22.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZHAOHONG YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/12/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/19/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 01/23/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) The assets on hand are exactly the same as the bank accounts listed on the corrected inventory and appraisal. Did the petitioner not open an interest-bearing bank account in the estate's name, as required by Probate Code section 9652? 2) The calculation of net cash available for final distribution is incorrect, unless the balance of the account 6066 is $213,606.28, the same amount as the corrected inventory and appraisal value. 3) The bank accounts identified by the decedent's mother in her disclaimer are not listed on the corrected inventory and appraisal and thus are not part of the assets on hand. Please explain why the decedent's mother should not be the sole distributee from whom a waiver of account must be obtained. 4) The omnibus clause must be corrected to identify the decedent's mother as the sole distributee, as the scope of her disclaimer was limited to known bank accounts.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS X. PERRINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Allowing Attorney Fees And Discharging Court Appointed Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/12/2024. 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the GC-020 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) If the LPS conservatorship is still in place, notice must be given to the LPS conservator (and the conservatee's attorney in the LPS proceeding, if any). LRSF 14.47(G)(1).
